---
name: Project VIC spec-kit issue
about: Plan a small, testable unit of work aligned to the Project VIC Cursor rules
title: ""
labels: "needs-spec"
assignees: ""
---

## Milestone

_Example: v0.1.0. The agent should align this issue to the reviewed milestone plan before implementation._

## User Story

As a _user role_, I want _capability_ so that _mission value_.

## Mission Outcome

_What mission problem does this issue help solve, and how will the user know it worked?_

## Background

_Why this issue matters. Include relevant context from `intake/problem-intake.md`, the milestone plan, or prior specs._

## Requirements

- 

## Four-Interface Impact

Mark each interface as `Affected`, `Not affected`, or `Deferred`, and explain why.

| Interface | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Human UI |  |  |
| Command-line / Power-user |  |  |
| Application / API / MCP |  |  |
| Interoperable Export |  |  |

## CASE/UCO/CAC Alignment

_Use CASE/UCO SDK v1.11.0+ and its MCP server. List relevant namespaces, classes, properties, relationships, controlled vocabularies, recipes, or validation expectations. If unknown, say what the agent must look up._

## Security and Compliance

_Default to NIST SP 800-53 and FBI CJIS Security Policy unless the user declares another national or agency framework. List applicable controls, sensitive-data boundaries, auth/audit/encryption/input-validation implications, and any questions for the user._

## Synthetic Test Data

_Describe safe synthetic data needed to test this issue. Do not use real case data, CSAM, suspected CSAM, credentials, agency-private information, or operational details._

## No-Stubs Implementation Expectations

_Describe what must be implemented for real. If any work is deferred, identify the tracked follow-up issue and the fail-closed behavior._

## Cross-Platform Considerations

_Note Linux, Windows 11, and macOS implications, including paths, scripts, containers, filesystem behavior, GPU/CPU assumptions, or setup differences._

## Brand and Design System Impact

_For visible surfaces, identify Project VIC brand/design-system token, component, terminology, accessibility, screenshot, report, or documentation implications. Say `Not applicable` if there is no visible surface._

## Performance and Auto-Research

_List measurable performance, quality, throughput, latency, memory, accuracy, storage, or test-duration metrics. If a baseline exists, note whether autoresearch should be offered to the user._

## Acceptance Criteria

- 

## Testing Notes

_How should this be tested with synthetic data? Include unit, integration, interface, export, security, ontology validation, or manual review expectations where applicable._

## Documentation and Release Impact

_List README, CHANGELOG, release notes, setup docs, issue/milestone updates, GitHub Actions, or user guidance that must change._

## Spec-Kit Readiness Checklist

- [ ] Issue is aligned to a milestone.
- [ ] User story and mission outcome are clear.
- [ ] Requirements are testable.
- [ ] Four-interface impact is documented.
- [ ] CASE/UCO/CAC lookup or mapping is documented.
- [ ] Security/compliance controls are identified.
- [ ] Synthetic test data is described.
- [ ] No-stubs expectations are explicit.
- [ ] Cross-platform impact is considered.
- [ ] Brand/design impact is considered for visible surfaces.
- [ ] Performance metrics and autoresearch opportunities are considered.
- [ ] Acceptance criteria and testing notes are sufficient for spec-kit planning.
